title: The Mischief Moles and the Dusty Bookshelf
summary: Two mischievous moles named Disco set out on a grand adventure across a vast rug filled with obstacles like Montessori blocks and a towering lion stool. After failing to jump over the furniture, they receive a flight to their destination: the dark, dusty realm beneath the bookshelf. Defying expectations of tidiness, the moles gather buckets of cat hair and dust from the floor to make their new home even messier, celebrating their successful mission of total chaos.

highlightQuote.text: Shed, shed, shed, the cats did the dust on all of the floor so you can get the dust and bring it under the bookshelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, put this blanket for me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I need that blanket on myself so I can get comfortable.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's go on. Tada! It's on you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Face out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Faces is out. And then what do we use for stars these days? Whoops. And then, turn off the lamp. It's time for stars and stories. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story with our mischief moles. Hey mischief moles, are you here?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm here, I'm here! says Disco and Disco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You and you mischief.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. But you know earlier today, says Disco, I met an amazing new kind of cat robot. You know what kind it was? It was a submarine cat robot.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, I explore under the chair and it was great. And do you want to explore under the bookshelf?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, I do! says Disco. Me too! says Disco. Those mischief moles, they want to explore.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And mischief under the bookshelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mhm. Let's just do mischief.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, under the bookshelf. It’ll have lots of mystery adventures on the way to the bookshelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so, Disco and Disco set out to do mischief under the bookshelf. But before they could get there they had to cross the big rug. And what problem did they have trying to get across? Something was in the way.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think anything was in the way.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Neither did they, but as they were walking, suddenly thump! Disco fell flat on his face. And then he got up and said, 'I must have fallen over something. Hey look out Disco!' But he was too late, because Disco said, 'Whoa, thump!' And he fell right down, too. So they both were tripping on something.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's just a pink block. I'll move it. I moved it back to Montessori school.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Disco said, 'Thank you for moving that block, we really fell right down on our faces because it tripped us up.' And so they kept going across the rug on their journey to get to the bookshelf. But then bonk! They bumped into something else.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's another Montessori block.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Another one! You gotta clean up this room, Isabella.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I already did clean up this room already. Oh, okay, and the cleaners did too yesterday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then, as they got almost all the way across the rug they encountered another obstacle, which was a big gray lion stool. And they said, 'What are we gonna do? How do we get past the lion stool?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We jump over it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco said, 'Okay, I'm gonna jump on the count of three. Count with me. One, two, three, boing!' And he jumped up as high as he could, but then bonk! He didn't make it all the way over and he slid back down. And Disco said, 'Let me try!' And he went 'One, two, three, bonk!' I didn't make it either, said Disco, because they were both just... they're only little moles, they're only kiddos, you know.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hey, how about I fly you to the bookshelf?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yay! said Disco. Hooray! said Disco. Let's fly! And did you make a sound while you flew them to the bookshelf?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I just did... I just went zzz as I flew them. Zzz. Yep, you're at the bookshelf, time to go under.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Disco and Disco went under the bookshelf and they said, 'Whoa, it's dark and dank and dusty and musty down here. A perfect place to do mischief.' And so what kind of mischief did they decide to do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You tell the story and then you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Disco said, 'Everyone expects us to clean the dust out from under this bookshelf, but you know what we're gonna do? We're gonna put even more dust under here!' We're gonna make it dustier and dustier and dustier. But wait, said Disco. Where are we gonna find the dust? And what did Disco say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shed, shed, shed, the cats did the dust on all of the floor so you can get the dust and bring it under the bookshelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the cats made the dust?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Shed made more dust.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's collect it. What are we gonna collect it in? We need something to carry it with.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I gave you that little bucket.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, the little bucket. We need two buckets. Okay, we got two buckets. And then we'll scoop the dust into the buckets. Ugh, this is hard work for mischief. And they did scoop, scoop, scoop, scoop, scoop, and they scooped the dust into the buckets until they were full. And then they dragged them, uff, uff, uff, back under the bookshelf and then dump! And all the dust poured out and made a big mess under the bookshelf. And they said, 'Ha ha! We didn't clean it, we dirtied it!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We did mischief! At the moles!

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Submarine Cat Robot
characters.description: An amazing new kind of cat robot that lives underwater.

characters.name: The Cats
characters.description: Dust-mantled creatures who shed fine sediment across the floors for the harvesting moles, awaiting the arrival of indoor greenery cultivated through the long winter snowstorms.

places.name: Under the Chair
places.description: A shadowed subterranean vault where the moles secure the spoils of their morning forage, illuminating piles of jagged, rust-eaten toys with the singular, sweeping beam of a flashlight.

places.name: Under the Bookshelf
places.description: The dark, dank, and dusty goal where the moles perform their main mischief.

places.name: The Big Rug
places.description: A treacherous area filled with obstacles like blocks and stools.

places.name: Montessori School
places.description: A leafy sanctuary of whispered learning where teachers like Miss Lamb decipher the secrets of the flora, this site also serves as the hallowed terminus where the enigmatic pink blocks are returned and reunited.
